summary: Translates a solver accuracy/order request and domain dimension into the stencil size, polynomial degree, spline degree, tree mode, and point set used by the RBF-FD assembler.

Normalizes a stencil backend specification into a callable stencil constructor. It accepts existing callables plus supported string aliases for RBF and weighted least-squares stencils, and raises for unknown backends.

Creates either a standard or overlapped finite-difference operator assembler using the requested stencil backend. It maps supported assembler aliases to FDDiffOp or FDODiffOp and rejects unknown names.
Evaluates a node-wise callback, array, or scalar and returns one floating-point value per node. Scalar values are broadcast and mismatched node counts raise an error.

Evaluates boundary condition values from a callback, array, or scalar, supporting callbacks that accept full boundary data or just boundary coordinates. It returns a flat array and validates that it has one value per boundary row.
Stacks the interior Poisson operator and boundary operator into one sparse system. For pure Neumann problems it augments the system with a constant-nullspace constraint row and column.
Concatenates the interior and boundary right-hand-side vectors for a Poisson solve. For pure Neumann systems it appends the zero constraint value used by the nullspace augmentation.
Converts several allowed initial-guess shapes into the full linear-system guess vector expected by the Poisson helpers. It also inserts boundary values and the Neumann nullspace variable when needed.
Solves a sparse linear system with GMRES using a supplied initial guess. If GMRES fails or returns non-finite values, it falls back to a direct sparse solve.
Attempts to build an incomplete-LU preconditioner for a sparse system and wrap it as a SciPy LinearOperator. It returns None if the factorization cannot be constructed.
Solves a sparse linear system with GMRES using optional initial guess and preconditioner inputs. It falls back to a direct sparse solve if the iterative solve fails or produces non-finite values.
Converts a state vector to a flat float array and checks that it has the expected physical-node length.
Detects callbacks that appear to depend only on coordinates by checking for a single positional argument. Non-callables and objects without code metadata are treated as not fixed.
Evaluates a boundary coefficient from a callback, array, or scalar, optionally passing time to callbacks that support it. Scalars are broadcast and the result must match the number of boundary nodes.
Evaluates a forcing term from a callback, array, or scalar using the callback signatures accepted by the time-dependent solvers. The result is flattened and must provide one value per physical row.
Evaluates time-dependent boundary values from flexible callback signatures or fixed values. It returns a flat boundary vector and validates that its length matches the boundary rows.
Builds the sparse linear system used by implicit diffusion steps by scaling the Laplacian, adding an identity block on physical rows, and appending boundary rows.
Builds all partition-of-unity patch data for a domain, including patch centers, radii, node memberships, stencils, and a center search tree.

Assembles a sparse partition-of-unity operator at query points by blending local patch RBF-FD weights. It supports interpolation, Laplacian, and boundary operators with optional normals and boundary coefficients.
Evaluates coefficient values at query points using partition-of-unity interpolation over local patch stencils. Points not covered by any patch fall back to nearest-node values.
Returns the patch-center spacing, using the provided spacing factor times the separation radius when positive and otherwise a default of two separation radii.
Returns the patch radius, using the provided radius factor times the separation radius when positive and otherwise a default of three separation radii.
Chooses patch centers greedily from the node set so accepted centers are separated by the requested spacing.
Finds the node indices belonging to each patch by radius search, with a nearest-neighbor fallback when too few nodes are found.
Computes the minimum number of nodes required per patch from the dimension and target order using twice the polynomial basis size plus one.
Creates stencil properties for local partition-of-unity patch stencils from the dimension and target order.
Initializes an RBFStencil on a given set of local patch nodes using the supplied stencil properties.
Builds one initialized RBFStencil for each patch node-index set.
Creates a PatchCenterTree containing a cKDTree searcher when patch centers are available, or an empty tree record otherwise.
Finds the patch indices whose centers are within the patch radius of each query point. If a query has no covering patch, it assigns the nearest patch when centers exist.
Computes local RBF-FD weights for interpolation, Laplacian, or mixed boundary operators on a single patch stencil.
Evaluates the compactly supported partition-of-unity patch weight polynomial for normalized distances.
Returns a new StencilProperties object containing the same stored fields as the input properties.
Initializes the diffusion solver by storing the domain, timestep, diffusion coefficient, and threading settings, then assembling the reusable Laplacian operator and clearing step history and caches.
Updates the time-step size and invalidates cached boundary and implicit solve data that depend on the old step size.
Seeds the BDF history with one physical state and clears higher-order history and cached solve data.
Seeds the BDF history with one, two, or three physical states so the solver can start at BDF1, BDF2, or BDF3 order.
Returns the latest available physical state from the stored BDF history.
Performs one first-order implicit BDF diffusion step with forcing and boundary data. It requires an initial state and advances through the shared implicit solve path.
Performs one second-order BDF diffusion step using the two most recent states and forcing at the requested time. It requires one previously completed step.
Performs one third-order BDF diffusion step using the three-state history and forcing at the requested time. It requires two previously completed steps.
Carries out the common implicit step workflow by evaluating boundary data, ensuring the linear system is ready, solving it, and rotating the BDF history.
Evaluates Neumann and Dirichlet boundary coefficient arrays, caching them when both callbacks appear time-independent.
Assembles the boundary operator for the current coefficients unless a reusable fixed-coefficient operator is already cached.
Clears the cached implicit system, preconditioner, and stored Laplacian scale.
Returns a cached implicit diffusion system for the requested Laplacian scale or builds it and its ILU preconditioner when needed.
Stores a newly computed state in the BDF history, rotating older states once third-order history is active.
Initializes one DiffusionSolver per species using the corresponding diffusion coefficient from the provided coefficient array.
Updates the shared time-step size and propagates it to every per-species diffusion solver.
Validates that the state matrix has one column per initialized species and seeds each species solver from its column.
Advances all species with first-order BDF diffusion using species-specific diffusion coefficients and callback wrappers.
Advances all species with second-order BDF diffusion using species-specific diffusion coefficients and callback wrappers.
Advances all species with third-order BDF diffusion using species-specific diffusion coefficients and callback wrappers.
Reports the distributed-state behavior of the first initialized per-species solver.
Returns the output range from the first species solver, or the domain physical-node range if no solvers exist.
Returns output nodes from the first species solver, or the domain physical nodes if no solvers exist.
Returns the Laplacian from the first species solver when available.
Returns the list of boundary operators currently stored by the per-species solvers.
Returns a copy of the per-species diffusion coefficient array.
Runs the requested BDF step for each species with callback adapters that pass species index and that species diffusion coefficient.
Initializes one PUDiffusionSolver per species using the corresponding diffusion coefficient from the provided coefficient array.
Updates the shared time-step size and propagates it to every per-species PU diffusion solver.
Validates that the state matrix has one column per initialized PU species solver and seeds each solver from its column.
Advances all species with first-order PU BDF diffusion using species-specific diffusion coefficients and callback wrappers.
Advances all species with second-order PU BDF diffusion using species-specific diffusion coefficients and callback wrappers.
Advances all species with third-order PU BDF diffusion using species-specific diffusion coefficients and callback wrappers.
Reports the distributed-state behavior of the first initialized PU species solver.
Returns the output range from the first PU species solver, or the domain physical-node range if no solvers exist.
Returns output nodes from the first PU species solver, or the domain physical nodes if no solvers exist.
Runs the requested BDF step for each PU species solver with callback adapters that pass species index and that species diffusion coefficient.
Stores the shared diffusion configuration for a multi-species solve and clears any existing per-species solvers.
Updates the shared time-step size and propagates it to all existing per-species diffusion solvers.
Validates a two-dimensional species state matrix, creates per-species solvers as needed, and seeds each solver from one state column.
Seeds each per-species diffusion solver with matching columns from one or more history matrices.
Advances all species with first-order BDF diffusion by dispatching column-wise forcing and boundary callbacks.
Advances all species with second-order BDF diffusion by dispatching column-wise forcing and boundary callbacks.
Advances all species with third-order BDF diffusion by dispatching column-wise forcing and boundary callbacks.
Reports the distributed-state behavior of the first per-species solver when solvers exist.
Returns the output range from the first per-species solver, or the domain physical-node range before solvers are created.
Returns output nodes from the first per-species solver, or the domain physical nodes before solvers are created.
Returns the Laplacian from the first per-species solver when available.
Returns the boundary operator from the first per-species solver when available.
Creates one configured DiffusionSolver per species on first use and rejects later calls with a different species count.
Runs the requested BDF step on each species solver using callbacks that extract that species column from matrix-valued forcing and boundary data.
Evaluates a state-dependent callback, scalar, or array using coordinates and the current state. Scalars are broadcast and the result must match the node count.
Builds a sparse rectangular diagonal matrix with the provided values on the leading diagonal and the requested column count.
Solves a Newton linear correction system with GMRES using an ILU preconditioner built from the provided preconditioner matrix.
Initializes the nonlinear variable-coefficient Poisson solver by building domain structures and assembling reusable Laplacian and gradient operators.
Sets the residual-norm tolerance used to decide nonlinear Newton convergence.
Sets the GMRES tolerance used for Newton correction solves.
Sets the maximum number of nonlinear iterations, forcing it to be at least one.
Evaluates boundary coefficients and values, assembles the boundary operator, and records whether the problem is pure Neumann.
Converts an optional initial guess into the full nonlinear unknown vector, including the Neumann Lagrange multiplier slot when needed.
Evaluates coefficient, coefficient derivative, forcing, and forcing derivative at the current state and builds the current variable PDE operator.
Builds the nonlinear residual from the PDE rows, boundary rows, and the pure-Neumann sum constraint when present.
Assembles the Newton Jacobian including derivatives of the coefficient field, gradient terms, forcing derivative, boundary rows, and optional Neumann augmentation.
Builds the sparse preconditioner matrix from the current PDE operator, forcing derivative, boundary rows, and optional Neumann augmentation.

Solves the nonlinear variable-coefficient Poisson problem with Newton iterations, GMRES linear solves, and a simple backtracking line search. It returns the converged solution, operators, boundary data, iteration count, and residual norm, or raises if convergence fails.
Reports that this solver returns local physical output values rather than a distributed full state.
Returns the zero-based output range covering the solver physical nodes.
Returns the physical nodes where the nonlinear Poisson solution is reported.
Returns the assembled Laplacian operator.
Returns copies of the assembled coordinate-gradient operators.
Returns the nonlinear PDE operator from the most recent residual assembly or solve.
Returns the boundary operator assembled for the most recent solve.
Reports whether the most recent nonlinear solve used pure-Neumann nullspace augmentation.
Returns the iteration index recorded for the most recent nonlinear solve.
Returns the residual norm recorded for the most recent nonlinear solve.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for set_nonlinear_tolerance.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for set_linear_tolerance.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for set_max_nonlinear_iterations.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for returns_distributed_state.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_output_range.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_output_nodes.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_laplacian.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_gradient_ops.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_last_pde_operator.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_bc_op.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for last_solve_used_nullspace.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_last_nonlinear_iterations.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_last_residual_norm.
Initializes the Poisson solver by building domain structures, collecting node geometry, and assembling the reusable Laplacian operator.

Solves a Poisson boundary-value problem with forcing and mixed boundary coefficients, adding a nullspace constraint for pure Neumann cases. It returns the physical solution plus assembled operators, right-hand sides, and solve metadata.
Returns the most recently assembled Laplacian operator.
Returns the boundary operator assembled during the most recent solve.
Reports whether the most recent Poisson solve used pure-Neumann nullspace augmentation.
Builds the implicit diffusion sparse system by scaling the localized Laplacian, adding identity on physical rows, and stacking boundary rows.
Initializes a partition-of-unity diffusion solver by building domain structures, patch geometry, a localized Laplacian, and empty state and solve caches.
Updates the time-step size and invalidates cached boundary and implicit solve data.
Seeds the PU diffusion BDF history with one validated physical state and clears higher-order history and caches.
Seeds the PU diffusion BDF history with one, two, or three validated physical states.
Returns the current physical state from the PU diffusion BDF history.
Reports that this solver returns values on its local physical output nodes rather than a distributed full state.
Returns the one-based output index range covering the PU diffusion physical nodes.
Returns the physical output nodes used by the PU diffusion solver.
Performs one first-order implicit PU diffusion step using localized operators, forcing, and boundary data.
Performs one second-order implicit PU diffusion step after one prior step is available.
Performs one third-order implicit PU diffusion step after two prior steps are available.
Runs the common PU implicit solve by updating boundary data, solving the localized diffusion system, and advancing BDF history.
Evaluates and optionally caches boundary coefficient arrays for the current time.
Builds or reuses the localized PU boundary operator for the current boundary coefficients.
Clears the cached PU implicit system, preconditioner, and Laplacian scale.
Returns a cached PU implicit system for the requested scale or builds the system and ILU preconditioner when needed.
Rotates and records the PU diffusion BDF state history after a successful solve.
Stores the shared PU diffusion configuration and clears any existing per-species PU diffusion solvers.
Updates the shared time-step size and propagates it to all existing per-species PU diffusion solvers.
Creates per-species PU diffusion solvers as needed and seeds each one from a column of the input state matrix.
Seeds each per-species PU diffusion solver with matching columns from the supplied history matrices.
Advances all species with first-order PU diffusion by dispatching column-wise forcing and boundary callbacks.
Advances all species with second-order PU diffusion by dispatching column-wise forcing and boundary callbacks.
Advances all species with third-order PU diffusion by dispatching column-wise forcing and boundary callbacks.
Reports that the multi-species PU diffusion solver returns local, non-distributed states.
Returns the output range from the first PU diffusion solver, or the domain physical-node range before solvers are created.
Returns output nodes from the first PU diffusion solver, or the domain physical nodes before solvers are created.
Creates one PUDiffusionSolver per species on first use and rejects later calls with a different species count.
Runs the requested BDF step on each species PU solver using callbacks that extract that species column from matrix-valued forcing and boundary data.
Creates the stencil properties used for semi-Lagrangian partition-of-unity interpolation patches.
Resets the advection boundary condition configuration to the unspecified mode with default tolerance and no inflow or periodic data.
Configures the advection boundary mode to require tangential flow at the boundary within the given normal-velocity tolerance.
Stores periodic advection boundary configuration and normal-velocity tolerance for later validation.
Configures inflow-Dirichlet advection boundary handling with a callback that supplies inflow values.
Returns the current advection boundary-condition configuration dictionary.
Reports that the PU semi-Lagrangian advection solver returns local output-node values rather than a distributed full state.
Returns the configured output index range for the advection solver.
Initializes the PU semi-Lagrangian advection solver by building domain structures, output nodes, patch centers, patch stencils, and a domain-measure estimate.
Returns the nodes where advected coefficients are represented and reported.
Samples an initial-condition callback at each output node and stores the result as a single-column coefficient array.
Creates a coefficient matrix filled with a constant value over all output nodes and requested columns.
Converts supplied nodal samples to a floating-point coefficient array without further projection.
Returns the supplied coefficients as a floating-point nodal-value array.
Evaluates the coefficient field at arbitrary points using localized partition-of-unity interpolation.
Runs one backward semi-Lagrangian advection step by tracing output nodes backward and sampling old coefficients with boundary handling.
Runs one forward semi-Lagrangian advection step with defect-correction iterations and optional global mass correction. Inflow-Dirichlet mode falls back to the backward step path.
Updates the semi-Lagrangian advection time-step size.
Resets counters that track how many forward defect-correction sweeps were used.
Returns a copy of the semi-Lagrangian solve-statistics counters.
Returns the number of partition-of-unity patches currently used by the advection solver.
Returns the number of advection degrees of freedom, equal to the number of output nodes.
Returns the patch radius used by the advection solver.
Returns the patch-center spacing used by the advection solver.
Estimates the total mass in one coefficient column as domain measure times the column mean.
Returns the stored estimate of the domain measure used for mass calculations.
Evaluates PU-SL coefficients at arbitrary query points by blending local patch interpolation weights, with nearest-node fallback for uncovered points.
Finds advection patch centers within the patch radius for each query point, using the tree when available and a nearest-patch fallback when none cover a query.
Traces points backward over one time step by delegating to the signed tracing helper with a negative step.
Traces points forward over one time step by delegating to the signed tracing helper with a positive step.
Advances points by a signed time step using a supplied Runge-Kutta callback when present, otherwise the built-in RK4 step.
Performs one classical fourth-order Runge-Kutta update for all points under the provided velocity field.
Applies the configured advection boundary condition to traced values, currently handling inflow-Dirichlet samples for outside points and rejecting periodic mode.
Checks that the configured advection boundary mode has the data the solver needs. It rejects periodic mode as unsupported and requires a callable inflow value for inflow-Dirichlet mode.
Tests whether a point is inside or close to the domain level set using a tolerance based on the domain separation radius.
Evaluates the velocity at one boundary point and returns its dot product with the supplied normal, validating the velocity shape.
Checks that the velocity field has negligible normal component on all boundary nodes for a tangential-flow boundary condition.
Evaluates the inflow-Dirichlet callback at given points and validates that it returns the expected point-by-column matrix shape.
Locates a level-set boundary crossing along a segment between an inside point and an outside point using bisection.
Adjusts forward semi-Lagrangian traced points and right-hand-side samples for boundary conditions, including inflow values and boundary-hit clipping.
Samples old coefficients at backward-traced points and substitutes inflow-Dirichlet boundary values for traces that leave the domain.
Adds a constant offset to each coefficient column so its estimated total mass matches the requested target mass.
Estimates the domain measure from the level set using a coarse grid in 2D or 3D, falling back to node count times spacing to the dimension when no level set is available.
Initializes the coupled PU-SL advection plus finite-difference diffusion solver, setting timestep, diffusion coefficient, transport direction, component solvers, and empty state history.
Updates the shared timestep on the coupled solver and propagates it to both advection and diffusion components.
Stores the requested transport direction string in lower case for later advection transport calls.
Compatibility helper that enables or disables the same mass constraint controlled by enable_mass_constraint.
Turns mass-constraint enforcement on or off and, when enabling without an explicit target, initializes the target from the current state if available.
Disables mass-constraint enforcement for future coupled steps.
Sets an explicit target mass and enables mass-constraint enforcement.
Stores one physical state as the starting history and initializes the mass target when mass conservation is enabled.
Seeds the coupled solver with one, two, or three physical history states and updates the completed-step count.
Returns the output nodes from the advection component.
Returns the output range from the advection component.
Reports the distributed-state behavior of the advection component.
Returns the PU-SL advection component used by the coupled solver.
Returns the finite-difference diffusion component used by the coupled solver.

Performs one first-order operator-split advection-diffusion step by transporting the previous state, solving a BDF1 diffusion step, optionally adding reaction forcing, and enforcing mass when configured.

Performs one second-order operator-split advection-diffusion step by transporting two history states, solving a BDF2 diffusion step, optionally extrapolating reaction forcing, and enforcing mass when configured.
Performs one third-order operator-split advection-diffusion step by transporting three history states, solving a BDF3 diffusion step, optionally extrapolating reaction forcing, and rotating history.
Returns the latest state in the coupled solver history.
Estimates the mass of a local state using the advection component mass calculation.
Transports a stored state over one or more timesteps using the configured forward or backward PU-SL advection direction, then optionally enforces the mass target.
Evaluates a forcing input at the coupled solver output nodes using the shared diffusion coefficient and requested time.
Builds a forcing callback that adds precomputed reaction values to the base forcing values.
Evaluates the base forcing callback and adds the captured reaction values after checking that the reaction vector matches the requested point count.
Evaluates a reaction callback using one of the supported signatures and validates that it returns one value per output point.
Initializes the mass-constraint target from a reference state when mass enforcement is enabled and no explicit target was set.
Adds a constant offset to a state so its estimated total mass matches the target mass.
Calls the parent first-order coupled advection-diffusion step while requiring and passing a reaction callback.
Calls the parent second-order coupled advection-diffusion step while requiring and passing a reaction callback.
Calls the parent third-order coupled advection-diffusion step while requiring and passing a reaction callback.
Delegates to the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver to reset boundary-condition configuration.
Delegates tangential-flow boundary configuration to the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Delegates periodic boundary configuration to the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Delegates inflow-Dirichlet boundary configuration to the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Returns the boundary-condition configuration from the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Reports the distributed-state behavior of the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Returns the output range from the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Initializes the multi-species PU-SL advection wrapper with a positive species count and initializes the shared scalar advection solver.
Returns the configured number of species columns.
Returns output nodes from the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Samples a vector-valued initial-condition callback at each output node and validates that it returns one value per species.
Creates a coefficient matrix filled with the same constant for every species and output node.
Creates a coefficient matrix by repeating one provided constant value per species across all output nodes.
Validates the species-column count and delegates sample projection to the underlying scalar advection solver.
Validates the species-column count and delegates nodal evaluation to the underlying scalar advection solver.
Validates the species-column count and delegates point evaluation to the underlying scalar advection solver.
Validates the species-column count and advances all species columns with the underlying forward PU-SL advection step.
Validates the species-column count and advances all species columns with the underlying backward PU-SL advection step.
Updates the time-step size on the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Resets solve-statistics counters on the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Returns solve-statistics counters from the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Returns the number of patches used by the underlying scalar PU-SL advection solver.
Returns the number of degrees of freedom per species from the underlying scalar solver.
Returns the total number of degrees of freedom across all species.
Returns the patch radius used by the underlying scalar solver.
Returns the patch spacing used by the underlying scalar solver.
Validates the species matrix and returns the estimated mass for the requested species column.
Validates the species matrix and returns estimated masses for all species columns.
Returns the domain-measure estimate from the underlying scalar solver.
Raises an error if the multi-species advection wrapper has not been initialized with a positive species count.
Checks that an input array has the configured number of species columns for the calling operation.
Initializes the coupled PU-SL advection plus PU diffusion solver, setting timestep, diffusion coefficient, transport direction, component solvers, and empty state history.
Updates the shared timestep on the coupled solver and propagates it to both advection and diffusion components.
Stores the requested transport direction string in lower case for later advection transport calls.
Compatibility helper that enables or disables the same mass constraint controlled by enable_mass_constraint.
Turns mass-constraint enforcement on or off and, when enabling without an explicit target, initializes the target from the current state if available.
Disables mass-constraint enforcement for future coupled steps.
Sets an explicit target mass and enables mass-constraint enforcement.
Stores one physical state as the starting history and initializes the mass target when mass conservation is enabled.
Seeds the coupled solver with one, two, or three physical history states and updates the completed-step count.
Returns the output nodes from the advection component.
Returns the output range from the advection component.
Reports the distributed-state behavior of the advection component.
Returns the PU-SL advection component used by the coupled solver.
Returns the partition-of-unity diffusion component used by the coupled solver.

Performs one first-order operator-split PU advection-diffusion step by transporting the previous state, solving a BDF1 PU diffusion step, optionally adding reaction forcing, and enforcing mass when configured.

Performs one second-order operator-split PU advection-diffusion step by transporting two history states, solving a BDF2 PU diffusion step, optionally extrapolating reaction forcing, and enforcing mass when configured.

Performs one third-order operator-split PU advection-diffusion step by transporting three history states, solving a BDF3 PU diffusion step, optionally extrapolating reaction forcing, and rotating history.
Returns the latest state in the coupled solver history.
Estimates the mass of a local state using the advection component mass calculation.
Transports a stored state over one or more timesteps using the configured forward or backward PU-SL advection direction, then optionally enforces the mass target.
Evaluates a forcing input at the coupled solver output nodes using the shared diffusion coefficient and requested time.
Builds a forcing callback that adds precomputed reaction values to the base forcing values.
Evaluates the base forcing callback and adds the captured reaction values after checking that the reaction vector matches the requested point count.
Evaluates a reaction callback using one of the supported signatures and validates that it returns one value per output point.
Initializes the mass-constraint target from a reference state when mass enforcement is enabled and no explicit target was set.
Adds a constant offset to a state so its estimated total mass matches the target mass.
Calls the parent first-order coupled PU advection-diffusion step while requiring and passing a reaction callback.
Calls the parent second-order coupled PU advection-diffusion step while requiring and passing a reaction callback.
Calls the parent third-order coupled PU advection-diffusion step while requiring and passing a reaction callback.
Builds the sparse variable-coefficient Poisson operator for -div(a grad u) from a Laplacian, gradient operators, and coefficient values.
Stacks a variable-coefficient PDE operator and boundary operator into one sparse system, optionally adding the pure-Neumann nullspace constraint row and column.
Initializes the variable-coefficient Poisson solver by building domain structures and assembling reusable Laplacian and coordinate-gradient operators.

Solves a positive scalar variable-coefficient Poisson problem with mixed boundary conditions and optional initial guess. It builds the variable PDE operator for the supplied coefficient field and returns the solution, operators, right-hand sides, and nullspace metadata.
Returns the assembled Laplacian operator.
Reports that the variable Poisson solver returns local physical output values rather than a distributed full state.
Returns the zero-based output range covering the solver physical nodes.
Returns the physical nodes where the variable Poisson solution is reported.
Returns copies of the assembled coordinate-gradient operators.
Returns the variable-coefficient PDE operator from the most recent solve.
Returns the boundary operator from the most recent solve.
Reports whether the most recent solve used pure-Neumann nullspace augmentation.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for returns_distributed_state.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_output_range.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_output_nodes.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_laplacian.
CamelCase compatibility wrapper for get_gradient_ops.
